metadata.scheme_name: Supply of School Stationery, Socks, Shoes, School Bag, etc. to all Students of Std I to VIII
metadata.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

raw_data.details: Provision of essential stationery items, socks, shoes and school bags to students in classes I to VIII of Government schools. This includes notebooks, a geometry box, writing instruments, an eraser, a sharpener, along with footwear and a school bag. It ensures that every child, regardless of their socio-economic background, has the basic physical tools required for a productive learning environment. The scheme covers all students from Standard I to VIII.
raw_data.benefits: 1. Reduces financial burden on parents by providing a comprehensive school kit. 2. Provision of free stationery kits includes school bag, pairs of socks, school shoes and a complete stationery set (notebooks, pens/pencils etc). 3. Providing shoes and socks promotes better foot health and hygiene for students commuting to school. 4. Promotes regular school attendance by supporting foundational needs.
raw_data.eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a student. 2. The applicant should be studying in class 1st to 8th. 3. The applicant should be studying in a government school in UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineNo separate application is required. Eligible students enrolled in government schools are automatically covered under the scheme.
raw_data.documents_required: No Documents required for this Scheme
raw_data.sources_and_references: [Supply Of School Stationery, Socks, School Bag, Etc To All Students Of Std I To VIII](https://ddd.gov.in/directorate-of-education/)
raw_data.scheme_name: Supply of School Stationery, Socks, Shoes, School Bag, etc. to all Students of Std I to VIII
raw_data.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

faqs.question: What is the purpose of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Reduces financial burden on parents by providing a comprehensive school kit.

faqs.question: Which students are eligible?
faqs.answer: Students from Classes 1 to 8 studying in government schools.

faqs.question: Are private school students eligible?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only for government school students.

faqs.question: Is the scheme for both boys and girls?
faqs.answer: Yes, it benefits both boys and girls equally.

faqs.question: How does the scheme address the issue of dropouts from Government schools?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to prevent dropouts by ensuring that students have access to necessary resources, thus promoting 100% enrolment and retention in Government schools.
